us-politics.headline: Trump Threatens Iran's Infrastructure, Sets Tuesday Deadline
us-politics.body:
Trump pressed a Tuesday night deadline on Iran, threatening to bomb civilian infrastructure if no deal is reached and suggesting the U.S. could charge tolls for Strait of Hormuz passage. He invoked God's support for the U.S. cause and brushed off questions about potential war crimes. OPEC+ has already agreed to boost output once the strait reopens, signaling markets are pricing in a resolution - or preparing for one.

The Pentagon canceled a scheduled press briefing with Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chair Caine, fueling speculation about internal tensions. A missing F-15 airman was rescued in Iran, a detail Trump highlighted publicly. House Democrat Sumayyah Ansari introduced articles of impeachment against Hegseth, citing the Iran conflict. The administration faces pressure from multiple directions as the deadline approaches.

sources.title: House Democrat says she’ll move to impeach Hegseth over Iran war
Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth
Trump says God supports U.S. cause in Iran war as he threatens wider bombing
5 takeaways from Trump’s news conference on Iran
Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
OPEC+ agrees to boost output when Strait of Hormuz reopens
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran
Trump doubles down on Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet US demands
Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded

us-politics.headline: GOP Pours Money Into 2026 Races as Democrats Face Headwinds
us-politics.body:
A Republican super PAC announced a $342 million investment in 2026 Senate races, while a separate AI-backed super PAC is expanding into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins. In Montana, Senator Steve Daines has handpicked Kurt Alme as his preferred successor, aiming to hold the seat. The early spending signals Republicans are treating the midterms as a consolidation opportunity rather than a defensive fight.

Democrats face their own turbulence. Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger's approval has dropped sharply amid backlash over redistricting. Former Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin signaled he has political ambitions still ahead. Rep. Anna Luna called on two House colleagues to resign over separate controversies.

sources.title: New poll reveals Spanberger's popularity is plummeting amid backlash over gerrymandering
Luna says Gonzales, Cherfilus-McCormick ‘need to go’
AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
Youngkin on political future: ‘I have more to give’
Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races

us-politics.headline: Trump Clashes With Courts, Rolls Back Rules on Multiple Fronts
us-politics.body:
Trump publicly attacked the Supreme Court over its handling of birthright citizenship and tariff cases, a rare direct broadside at the judiciary from a sitting president. The EPA took initial steps to roll back methane flaring rules, drawing criticism from environmental groups who say the move undermines climate commitments. The Education Department rescinded Title IX agreements with schools that had extended protections based on gender identity.

Senator Mullin pressed cities with international airports to cooperate with federal immigration enforcement, warning of consequences for those that refuse. Arizona Republicans forced a formal probe into one county's anti-ICE policies. The moves reflect a coordinated push to tighten enforcement across agencies - and set up legal and political fights heading into the midterms.

sources.title: Mullin to cities with international airports: ‘You’ve got to partner with us’
EPA takes early step in methane rule rollback
Trump rips Supreme Court over birthright citizenship, tariffs
Arizona Republicans force probe of county’s anti-ICE policies, putting Democrat AG on the clock
Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity

us-politics.headline: Obama Center ID Rules Draw Hypocrisy Charges; Mamdani Plan Faces DOJ
us-politics.body:
The Obama Presidential Center drew criticism after requiring photo ID for free entry - a policy critics say contradicts Democrats' longstanding opposition to voter ID laws. Supporters argue the two contexts are not comparable, but the optics handed conservatives a ready-made talking point. Fox News and others amplified the contrast sharply.

New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani unveiled a racial equity plan that drew an immediate pushback from the Department of Justice. The DOJ's swift response signals the administration is willing to challenge local equity initiatives directly. Separately, H2A agricultural visa changes face rare bipartisan opposition, with both conservatives worried about labor market effects and liberals concerned about worker protections.

sources.title: Obama Center takes heat as critics cry foul over ID rules for free entry — while Dems blast voter ID laws
Mamdani unveils new 'racial equity plan' for more 'equitable future' that prompts quick DOJ pushback
Conservatives and liberals oppose H2A visa changes for immigrant farm workers

au-politics.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith arrested, faces five war crimes murder charges
au-politics.body:
Former SAS soldier and Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith has been arrested and is expected to be charged with five counts of murder relating to alleged war crimes in Afghanistan. The charges follow years of investigation by the Australian Federal Police after a landmark 2023 civil defamation case in which a Federal Court judge found allegations of unlawful killings against him were substantially proven.

The Australian War Memorial has announced it will review his display following the arrest. Roberts-Smith has consistently denied wrongdoing. The case marks the first time an Australian soldier has faced criminal war crimes charges of this scale, and will test the limits of Australia's war crimes prosecution framework.

sources.title: News live: Australians buy record number of new EVs in March; Australian War Memorial to review Ben Roberts-Smith display
Ben Roberts-Smith arrested: former Australian soldier to be charged with five war crime murders in Afghanistan
Ben Roberts-Smith expected to be charged with war crimes

au-politics.headline: Australia sidesteps Trump's Iran war blame game
au-politics.body:
Prime Minister Anthony Albanese deflected when asked about Donald Trump's claim that Australia "didn't help" during US military action against Iran, saying it was "not a question for me." Trump has offered shifting and at times contradictory accounts of allied involvement in the conflict, undermining his own credibility on the issue according to analysts.

The diplomatic friction comes as the Iran war continues to ripple through global energy markets. Fuel prices have surged in Australia, triggering panic buying at petrol stations - with some customers filling everything from Coke bottles to 200-litre drums - and driving a sharp spike in EV sales as buyers seek alternatives to petrol.

sources.title: 'That's not a question for me': Albanese on Trump saying Australia 'didn't help' Iran war – video
Trump's shifting Iran war rhetoric is working against his credibility
EV sales surge as fuel anxiety clears auctions, showrooms in Australia
Coke bottles to a 200L drum: Petrol stations see dangerous fuel stockpiling

au-politics.headline: Bondi nanny to be extradited to Chile over Pinochet-era crimes
au-politics.body:
Adriana Rivas, a former Sydney nanny accused of being an agent of Augusto Pinochet's secret police, has lost her bid to avoid extradition and will be sent to Chile to face kidnapping charges. Rivas, who lived in Bondi for years, is alleged to have participated in the abduction and disappearance of political opponents during Chile's military dictatorship in the 1970s.

Australian courts rejected her extradition challenge. The case has drawn attention to how former operatives of authoritarian regimes have lived quietly in Australia for decades.

sources.title: Ex-Bondi nanny fails in bid to avoid extradition over Chilean kidnapping claims
Accused Pinochet agent turned Bondi nanny Adriana Rivas to be extradited to Chile

au-politics.headline: NT gambling bill, Melbourne rubbish strike, Queensland crime drop
au-politics.body:
The Northern Territory parliament is debating a gambling reforms bill that critics, including industry figures, have labelled a "kneejerk" reaction to a recent Four Corners investigation into conflicts of interest at the Racing and Wagering Commission. Supporters say the bill addresses genuine governance failures.

In Melbourne, council workers have walked off the job across multiple municipalities in a wage dispute, halting rubbish collection for residents. Meanwhile, new Queensland government data shows crime offences per capita have fallen, though raw victim numbers remain a point of contention between the government and opposition ahead of ongoing law-and-order debates.

sources.title: Gambling reforms bill slammed as 'kneejerk' response to Four Corners
Council workers stop collecting rubbish across Melbourne over wage dispute
New data on crime in Queensland shows drop in offences per capita

world.headline: Trump's Iran Ultimatum Nears Deadline With No Deal in Sight
world.body:
US and Israeli strikes have hit Iranian power plants, nuclear facilities including Bushehr, and residential areas, with rescuers still pulling survivors from rubble. Trump has threatened further devastating attacks while simultaneously claiming Iranians are begging for the bombing to continue - a framing analysts and Iranian officials have flatly rejected. The Strait of Hormuz remains a flashpoint. Trump floated charging foreign ships for passage through the waterway, raising alarm over global oil supply chains.

Hezbollah and the Houthis have joined Iran in striking Israel, widening the conflict across the region. A downed US airman was rescued from a remote part of Iran in a covert operation whose details Trump threatened to jail a reporter for leaking. North Korea, notably, is keeping its distance from Tehran.

sources.title: Trump’s threat to strike Iran’s power plants looms
‘Why would Iran agree to a ceasefire given US and Israeli track record?’
Iran war live: Trump warns of attacks as Hormuz deal deadline nears
Clock ticks on Trump's Iran ultimatum with little sign of breakthrough
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war
Why is Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant being attacked?
Lebanon’s Hezbollah and Yemen’s Houthis join Iran in strike on Israel
Trump claims Iranians begging US to keep bombing Iran
Video: Trump threatens to jail reporter over Iran airman rescue leak
Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
North Korea keeping Iran at arm’s length, reports Seoul

world.headline: Israel Strikes Lebanon and Gaza as Civilian Toll Mounts
world.body:
Israeli forces have intensified strikes across Lebanon and Gaza, deepening a ground offensive that analysts say is deliberately designed to fracture Lebanese society along sectarian lines. At least ten Palestinians were killed near a Gaza school in a single strike, with separate clashes between Hamas and a militia adding to the death toll. Protesters stormed the US embassy in Tel Aviv demanding an end to the wars.

In Hebron, Israeli soldiers fired tear gas at Palestinian youth during a raid. The pattern of strikes - targeting civilian infrastructure and densely populated areas - is drawing renewed international criticism, though no ceasefire talks appear imminent.

sources.title: ‘They want to create a rift’: Israeli attacks deepen Lebanon fissures
Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say

world.headline: Vance Flies to Budapest to Boost Orban Before Sunday Vote
world.body:
US Vice President JD Vance is visiting Hungary ahead of Sunday's election, offering a visible show of White House support for Viktor Orban. The trip is the clearest signal yet that the Trump administration views Orban's brand of nationalist politics as a model worth backing inside the EU. Orban faces a tighter race than usual against opposition challenger Peter Magyar.

The visit has unsettled European capitals already wary of Washington's alignment with far-right governments across the continent.

sources.title: JD Vance due in Hungary to back Orban's re-election bid
Vance heads to Budapest to shore up Orban’s support before Sunday vote

world.headline: Taiwan Opposition Leader Visits China for First Time Since 2016
world.body:
Taiwan's main opposition leader has arrived in China for the first visit by a senior Taiwanese political figure in nearly a decade. The trip signals a potential opening in cross-strait relations, though the ruling Democratic Progressive Party has distanced itself from the visit and warned it does not represent official Taiwanese policy. Beijing has framed the visit as evidence of support for reunification dialogue.

Separately, a new analysis finds China leading the US in some AI benchmarks while the US holds advantages in others - with the gap between the two powers narrowing fast in both directions.

sources.title: Taiwan opposition leader makes first China visit since 2016
China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead

ai.headline: OpenAI insiders distrust Altman as company eyes AI economy
ai.body:
Internal tensions at OpenAI are spilling into public view, with insiders describing Sam Altman as the core problem facing the company. The criticism centers on trust, not capability - a distinction that matters as OpenAI pushes an ambitious economic vision that includes public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek to manage AI-driven job displacement.

Separately, a group of OpenAI alumni have been quietly deploying a fund that could reach $100 million, signaling that the talent and capital networks built around the company are now branching out independently. The combination of internal friction and external defection suggests OpenAI's grip on its own ecosystem is loosening even as it tries to shape global AI policy.

sources.title: “The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund

ai.headline: Iran threatens Stargate data centers as AI infrastructure goes geopolitical
ai.body:
Iran has issued threats against OpenAI's Stargate data centers, specifically targeting the facility under development in Abu Dhabi. The threat puts a spotlight on how concentrated AI infrastructure has become - and how that concentration creates hard geopolitical targets.

Anthropic is moving in the same direction, expanding its compute partnership with Google and Broadcom to build next-generation infrastructure. As the major AI labs race to lock in hardware capacity, the physical footprint of AI is growing faster than the security frameworks around it.

sources.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-gen compute

ai.headline: Robotics hits 99% reliability as AI consulting undercuts McKinsey
ai.body:
A new general-purpose robotics model called GEN-1 is claiming 99% task reliability across physical jobs ranging from folding boxes to repairing vacuums. That figure, if it holds in independent testing, would mark a meaningful threshold - the point where robots become viable for production environments rather than just demos.

On the white-collar side, Indian startup Rocket is pitching AI-generated strategy reports styled after McKinsey deliverables, at a fraction of the cost. Together, the two stories sketch the same pressure hitting different parts of the labor market: automation moving from plausible to practical.

sources.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost
The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI

ai.headline: Vibe coding backlash grows as AI music floods iTunes chart
ai.body:
A sharp critique of vibe coding - the practice of building software almost entirely through AI prompts - is gaining traction, with critics arguing it produces brittle, unreviewed code that developers don't actually understand. The debate cuts to a real question about where AI assistance ends and engineering judgment begins.

The same dynamic is playing out in music. An AI-generated artist named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart despite having no human behind the name. Both cases point to the same pattern: AI output flooding distribution channels faster than any quality or authenticity filter can respond.

sources.title: The cult of vibe coding is dogfooding run amok
AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart

btc.headline: Bitcoin slides toward $68K as whales sell and options hedge
btc.body:
Bitcoin is under pressure, dropping toward $68,000 as whale selling accelerates and retail demand thins out. The options market is quietly pricing in a major downside move, suggesting sophisticated traders are hedging against further losses rather than buying the dip.

Yet the picture is mixed. ETF inflows just hit their highest level since February, and Strategy is reportedly absorbing nearly three times the new BTC supply entering the market - a dynamic some analysts say could push prices back toward $110,000. Major tokens including ether and solana held relatively steady as Trump set a Tuesday deadline on Iran nuclear talks, adding a geopolitical wildcard to an already tense market.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETF inflows hit highest level since February
Bitcoin drops toward $68,000 as demand weakens and whales sell
Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move
Bitcoin, ether, solana hold steady as Trump sets Tuesday night deadline for Iran deal

btc.headline: SEC drafts 'reg crypto' framework as safe harbor heads to White House
btc.body:
U.S. crypto regulation is moving fast on two fronts. SEC Chair Atkins says the agency is close to releasing a dedicated fundraising framework - dubbed 'reg crypto' - to give token issuers clearer rules for raising capital. Separately, a crypto market safe harbor proposal has landed at the White House for final review.

The back-to-back developments signal a deliberate pivot from the enforcement-first approach of the previous administration. How quickly either measure becomes binding policy remains unclear, but the direction is unmistakable.

sources.title: Crypto market safe harbor lands at White House for review
SEC close to putting out 'reg crypto' for fundraising questions, Chair Atkins says

btc.headline: AI giants muscle into cheap power, squeezing Bitcoin miners
btc.body:
Bitcoin miners now have a well-funded rival for the cheap electricity they depend on. Anthropic has signed a multi-gigawatt compute deal that puts AI data centers in direct competition with mining operations for low-cost power contracts - a fight miners are increasingly ill-equipped to win on price alone.

Meanwhile, Grayscale argues that Bitcoin's quantum computing threat is more a social coordination problem than a technical one, pointing out that migrating billions in dormant coins to quantum-resistant addresses requires human consensus, not just a software patch. Both stories underscore how external pressures - energy markets and cryptographic obsolescence - are quietly reshaping Bitcoin's long-term risk profile.

sources.title: Bitcoin miners face a new rival for cheap power as Anthropic signs multi-gigawatt compute deal
Bitcoin’s quantum challenges are ‘more social than technical’: Grayscale

btc.headline: Onchain data convicts terrorists; Solana tightens DeFi defenses
btc.body:
Blockchain's transparency is proving useful in court. TRM Labs reports that onchain evidence was central to convicting three terrorism financiers in Indonesia, marking one of the clearest examples yet of crypto's immutable transaction trail being used as prosecutorial evidence.

On the security side, the Solana Foundation is rolling out new tools and an incident response network to harden DeFi protocols after a string of attacks. Separately, risk manager Chaos Labs has exited its role at Aave, saying the decision was not made hastily - leaving one of DeFi's largest lending protocols searching for a new risk partner at a volatile moment.

sources.title: Onchain evidence was key to convicting 3 terrorism financiers: TRM Labs
Solana Foundation looks to beef up DeFi security as attacks continue
Chaos Labs taps out as Aave's risk provider, decision ‘not made in haste’

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised white fish marinated in sweet miso and sake, broiled until lacquered and tender
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy dumbbells, open floor space
exercise.description: Cycle through farmer carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries for 40-metre lengths, resting only between variations — brutally effective for grip, core stability, and real-world strength

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same thing — philosophers have extended this to human cells, memory, and consciousness, spawning centuries of debate from Hobbes to Parfit
learn.why: Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' argues personal identity is less fixed than we assume, which quietly dismantles how most people think about self-improvement, regret, and responsibility — unsettling and genuinely useful
